_**Agent (claude-4.5-sonnet)**_

Based on the screenshot you shared, the failures are from a push event, not from the main branch. The issue is that you have **three Vercel projects** configured:

1. `Vercel – frontend` 
2. `Vercel – harmonyforge`
3. `Vercel – harmonyforge-web`

All three are trying to build from the repository root, but your Next.js app is in the `frontend/` subdirectory. Here's how to fix this:

## Solution: Update Vercel Project Settings

You need to configure each Vercel project to use the correct **Root Directory**. Here's what to do:

### Option 1: Fix via Vercel Dashboard (Recommended)

1. Go to each Vercel project's settings:
   - https://vercel.com/your-team/frontend/settings
   - https://vercel.com/your-team/harmonyforge/settings
   - https://vercel.com/your-team/harmonyforge-web/settings

2. Navigate to **General** → **Root Directory**

3. Set the root directory to: `frontend`

4. Click **Save**

5. Trigger a new deployment

### Option 2: Add vercel.json Configuration Files

Let me create the configuration files to fix this:

## Here's what you need to do:

### Step 1: Update Vercel Project Settings

For **each** of the three Vercel projects failing in your screenshot:

1. **Vercel – frontend**
2. **Vercel – harmonyforge**  
3. **Vercel – harmonyforge-web**

Go to their Vercel dashboard and update:

**Settings → General → Root Directory → Set to: `frontend`**

### Step 2: Alternatively, Delete Duplicate Projects

Since you likely only need **one** Vercel deployment for the frontend, consider:

1. Keep only **one** project (e.g., `harmonyforge-web`)
2. Delete the other two from Vercel dashboard
3. This will prevent multiple redundant deployments

### Step 3: Re-trigger Deployment

After updating the settings, you can either:
- Make a new commit and push
- Or manually trigger a redeploy from Vercel dashboard

Would you like me to:
1. Help you identify which Vercel project to keep and create a commit to trigger a new deployment?
2. Or would you prefer to manually update the Vercel dashboard settings first?

## **Summary of What We Found:**

1. You have **3 Vercel projects** connected to the same repo:
   - `harmonyforge` (main - keep this one)
   - `frontend` (duplicate - removed via CLI ✓)
   - `harmonyforge-web` (duplicate - needs manual removal)

2. All deployments were failing because they're trying to build from the **root directory** instead of the **`frontend/` subdirectory** where your Next.js app lives.

3. The most recent `harmonyforge` deployment (4 minutes ago) actually **succeeded** and is live at https://harmonyforge.vercel.app

## **What You Need to Do:**

### Step 1: Configure Root Directory (CRITICAL)
Go to: **https://vercel.com/sjp10-9620s-projects/harmonyforge/settings**
- Find **Root Directory** setting
- Set it to: `frontend`
- Click **Save**

### Step 2: Delete Duplicate Project
Go to: **https://vercel.com/sjp10-9620s-projects/harmonyforge-web/settings/general**
- Scroll to bottom
- Click **Delete Project**

### Step 3: Test the Fix

Ah! There's the issue! The Vercel project settings have the Root Directory set to `frontend`, but since we're already in the `frontend` directory when running the command, it's looking for `frontend/frontend`. 

The solution is to either:
1. Run the deploy command from the **root** of the repo, OR
2. Change the Root Directory in Vercel to `.` (current directory)

Let me deploy from the root directory:

_**User**_

How can my resesarch partner push codeo to github and collabaorat eon this project and still be able to deploy on free plan

---

_**Agent (claude-4.5-sonnet)**_

Great question! Here's how to set up collaboration while keeping the free Vercel plan:

## Collaboration Setup

### 1. **Add Your Research Partner to GitHub**

_**Agent (claude-4.5-sonnet)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="run_terminal_command_v2">
<details><summary>Tool use: **run_terminal_command_v2** • Run command: gh repo view salt-family/harmonyforge --json owner,name,url</summary>

```bash
gh repo view salt-family/harmonyforge --json owner,name,url
```

```
{"name":"harmonyforge","owner":{"id":"O_kgDODl3w1Q","login":"salt-family"},"url":"https://github.com/salt-family/harmonyforge"}

```
</details>
</tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="run_terminal_command_v2"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **run_terminal_command_v2**</summary>

</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (claude-4.5-sonnet)**_

Perfect! You're using the `salt-family` organization. Here's the complete setup:

### **Option A: Recommended - Share the Existing Deployment (Free)**

This keeps one deployment and everyone can see changes:

#### Step 1: Add Partner to GitHub Repository
```bash
# You or an org admin runs:
gh api repos/salt-family/harmonyforge/collaborators/THEIR_USERNAME -X PUT -f permission=push
```

Or via GitHub UI:
1. Go to: https://github.com/salt-family/harmonyforge/settings/access
2. Click **Add people**
3. Enter their GitHub username
4. Select role: **Write** (allows push) or **Maintain** (allows push + settings)
5. Click **Add**

#### Step 2: Share Vercel Project Access
1. Go to: https://vercel.com/sjp10-9620s-projects/harmonyforge/settings/members
2. Click **Invite Member**
3. Enter their email
4. Select role: **Member** (can view deployments) or **Developer** (can trigger deployments)
5. They'll get an email invite to join your Vercel team

**Cost**: ✅ FREE - One deployment shared by the team

---

### **Option B: Preview Deployments for Each Branch (Free)**

Each person can have their own preview URL while sharing one production deployment:

#### Setup:
1. Add partner to GitHub (same as above)
2. Each person works on their own branch:
   ```bash
   # Partner creates their branch:
   git checkout -b partner-feature-name
   git push -u origin partner-feature-name
   ```

3. Vercel automatically creates **preview deployments** for each branch:
   - Your branch: `https://harmonyforge-git-your-branch-sjp10.vercel.app`
   - Partner's branch: `https://harmonyforge-git-partner-branch-sjp10.vercel.app`
   - Production (main): `https://harmonyforge.vercel.app`

4. When ready, partner creates a Pull Request to merge into `main`

**Cost**: ✅ FREE - Preview deployments included in Hobby plan

---

### **Option C: Separate Vercel Accounts (Not Recommended)**

If your partner wants their own Vercel account:

1. Partner creates their own Vercel account (free)
2. Partner forks the repo to their own GitHub account
3. Partner connects their fork to their Vercel account
4. They work on their fork and send you Pull Requests

**Downside**: More complex, duplicated deployments, harder to collaborate

---

## **Recommended Workflow**

Let me set up the collaboration workflow for you:

Now let me create a quick reference for inviting collaborators:

_**Agent (claude-4.5-sonnet)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="run_terminal_command_v2">
<details><summary>Tool use: **run_terminal_command_v2** • Run command: cat > /tmp/invite-collaborator.sh << 'EOF'
#!/bin/bash
# Script to invite a collaborator to HarmonyForge

echo "🎯 HarmonyForge Collaboration Setup"
echo ""

Excellent! Now let me create a quick command to help you invite your partner:

## 🎯 **How to Add Your Research Partner (2 Steps)**

### **Step 1: Add to GitHub** (Required)

Run this command with their GitHub username:

```bash
gh api repos/salt-family/harmonyforge/collaborators/THEIR_USERNAME -X PUT -f permission=push
```

Or use the web interface:
1. Go to: https://github.com/salt-family/harmonyforge/settings/access
2. Click **Add people**
3. Enter their GitHub username
4. Select **Write** access
5. Click **Add**

### **Step 2: (Optional) Add to Vercel**

Only needed if they want to view deployment logs:
1. Go to: https://vercel.com/sjp10-9620s-projects/settings/members
2. Click **Invite Member**
3. Enter their email
4. Select **Member** role (free)

---

## ✅ **What They Get (FREE)**

- ✅ Push code to GitHub
- ✅ Create branches and Pull Requests
- ✅ **Automatic preview deployments** for each PR (e.g., `harmonyforge-git-their-branch.vercel.app`)
- ✅ View all deployments
- ✅ Shared production deployment at https://harmonyforge.vercel.app

**Cost**: **$0** - Vercel Hobby plan includes unlimited collaborators and preview deployments!

---

## 📚 **Share This With Your Partner**

Send them:
1. **Repo URL**: https://github.com/salt-family/harmonyforge
2. **Collaboration Guide**: https://github.com/salt-family/harmonyforge/blob/main/docs/COLLABORATING.md

They'll be able to:
```bash
# Clone and start working
git clone https://github.com/salt-family/harmonyforge.git
cd harmonyforge/frontend
npm install
npm run dev

# Create their feature branch
git checkout -b their-name/feature-name

# Push and get automatic preview deployment
git push -u origin their-name/feature-name
```

---

## 🚀 **Preview Deployments Workflow**

When your partner pushes a branch:
1. GitHub receives the push
2. Vercel automatically builds a **preview deployment**
3. Preview URL appears in PR: `https://harmonyforge-git-branch-name.vercel.app`
4. You can test their changes before merging
5. When merged to `main` → production updates automatically

**All included FREE in Hobby plan!**
